description: Running terraform force-unlock to clear a state lock while another apply is actually in progress, racing two writers into state corruption.
body: Failure mode: a run seems stuck, so an operator force-unlocks the state, but another apply is genuinely still running; both now write state and corrupt it or apply conflicting changes. Detection signals: state shows impossible/partial resource sets after two near-simultaneous runs; the 'stuck' lock belonged to a live job; later plans show phantom drift. Do not force-unlock without confirming no active run holds the lock. Safer fallback: back up state first, verify via the backend/CI that no run is active, force-unlock only the confirmed-abandoned lock id, then plan to validate state integrity.
